introduction: This spec defines the Go SDK client library for the apikit framework — a typed HTTP client that wraps every built-in API endpoint as a Go method on a `Client` struct, living in the root `apikit` package and serving as the exclusive API client layer for the CLI (specs 13-15).

glossary.APIError: A typed error struct matching the server's error envelope (code + message). Implements the error interface. Error() returns "API error {Code}: {Message}". Callers inspect it via errors.As.
glossary.APIKeyFull: The full API key object returned on creation or refresh, including the secret key value (key, key_id, expires_at).
glossary.APIKeyMeta: The metadata-only API key object returned in listings (key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at). Does not include the secret key value.
glossary.AuthCallbackRequest: The request body for POST /auth/callback containing provider, code, redirect_uri, and optional expires.
glossary.AuthCallbackResponse: The response body from POST /auth/callback containing a User and an APIKeyFull.
glossary.Client: The SDK's entry point — a struct that holds configuration (base URL, API key, HTTP client, mount point) and provides methods for each API endpoint. Safe for concurrent use after construction.
glossary.ClientOption: A function type `func(*Client)` used in the functional options pattern to configure a Client at construction time.
glossary.ErrNotModified: A sentinel error returned when a conditional GET receives HTTP 304 (Not Modified). Callers check with errors.Is. The accompanying *Response[T] is nil.
glossary.HealthResponse: The response body from health probe endpoints (/healthz, /readyz), containing a status string.
glossary.ListOrgsOptions: A struct that configures the ListOrgs request, including IncludeBlocked bool. Designed to grow with additional filter fields in future iterations.
glossary.ListUsersOptions: A struct that configures the ListUsers request, including IncludeBlocked bool. Designed to grow with additional filter fields in future iterations.
glossary.NewClient: The constructor function that creates a new Client given a baseURL and zero or more ClientOption values.
glossary.OAuthProvider: A provider discovery object returned by GET /auth/providers, containing name and authorize_url.
glossary.OrgMember: An informal alias for User used in the master PRD to describe users in the context of organization membership. Not a distinct Go type; member listing endpoints return []*User.
glossary.PAT: Personal Access Token — the metadata-only token object returned in listings (token_id, name, permissions, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at).
glossary.PATFull: The full personal access token object returned on creation, including the secret token value (token, token_id, name, permissions, expires_at).
glossary.RequestOption: A function type `func(*http.Request)` used to configure per-request behavior such as conditional GET headers.
glossary.Response[T]: A generic wrapper that pairs a typed API response with HTTP headers, enabling ETag access without losing type safety. Non-nil only on successful (2xx) responses; nil on 304.
glossary.RevokeKeyResponse: The response body from DELETE /user/keys/:key_id, containing key_id and revoked_at.
glossary.VersionResponse: The response body from GET /version, containing version, build_time, commit, and mount_point.
glossary.WithAPIKey: A ClientOption that sets the Bearer token for the Authorization header on every request.
glossary.WithHTTPClient: A ClientOption that overrides the default http.Client, enabling custom timeouts, TLS config, and transport.
glossary.WithIfNoneMatch: A RequestOption that adds an If-None-Match header to a single request for conditional GET.
glossary.WithMountPoint: A ClientOption that overrides the default mount point (/api/v1). The path is normalized at construction time.
glossary.WithRequestID: A ClientOption that sets a static X-Request-ID header on every request.
glossary.apikit: The root Go package containing both the server framework and the SDK client library.
glossary.bare JSON array: A JSON response body that is a top-level array (e.g., [{...}]), as opposed to a wrapped object (e.g., {"items": [...]}). All list endpoints in this API return bare arrays.
glossary.baseURL: The server's root URL (e.g., https://api.example.com). Passed to NewClient. All endpoint URLs are constructed relative to this. Trailing slash is stripped at construction time.
glossary.canonical shared types: The domain object structs (User, Organization, etc.) defined in sdk_types.go. These are the single authoritative definitions used by both the SDK client and server-side handlers in the apikit package.
glossary.conditional GET: An HTTP GET request with an If-None-Match header containing a previously received ETag. The server returns 304 if the resource is unchanged.
glossary.do: The internal method on Client that handles request construction, header setting, execution, and response decoding for all endpoint methods.
glossary.functional options: A Go pattern where configuration is applied via variadic function arguments (NewClient(url, opts...)), enabling extensibility without breaking changes.
glossary.mount point: The URL path prefix for API endpoints (default /api/v1). Configurable via WithMountPoint. Normalized at construction time: leading slash added if missing, trailing slash stripped. Health probes are outside the mount point.
glossary.sdk_types.go: The source file in the apikit package that contains all canonical shared domain object type definitions used by both the SDK client and server-side handlers.
glossary.url.Values: The net/url standard library type used for query parameter construction, ensuring correct encoding and forward-compatible extension.

user_story.goal: construct an API client with a single function call using the functional options pattern
user_story.benefit: I can configure authentication, HTTP settings, and mount point without a complex config struct

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil *Client with default httpClient set to http.DefaultClient, mountPoint set to "/api/v1", apiKey set to empty string, and requestID set to empty string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Client (never nil, even for empty baseURL)

acceptance_criteria.action: strips a trailing slash from baseURL at construction time before storing it on the Client struct
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.baseURL has no trailing slash

acceptance_criteria.action: is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines after construction — all fields are set once in NewClient and never modified thereafter; no synchronization is required by callers
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no data races under concurrent method calls

acceptance_criteria.action: sets the Client's apiKey field to the provided key string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.apiKey equals the provided key
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption

acceptance_criteria.action: sets the Client's httpClient field to the provided *http.Client, overriding http.DefaultClient
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.httpClient equals the provided *http.Client
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption

acceptance_criteria.action: sets the Client's requestID field to the provided id string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.requestID equals the provided id
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption

acceptance_criteria.action: prepends a leading slash to the path before storing it as the Client's mountPoint
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.mountPoint starts with "/"
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption with a path that lacks a leading slash

acceptance_criteria.action: strips the trailing slash from the path before storing it as the Client's mountPoint
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.mountPoint has no trailing slash
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption with a path that has a trailing slash

acceptance_criteria.action: normalizes the empty string to "/" and stores it as the Client's mountPoint
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Client.mountPoint equals "/"
acceptance_criteria.trigger: applied as a ClientOption with an empty string

edge_cases.action: accepts the empty string without panicking or returning an error; the returned *Client is non-nil; requests will fail at the HTTP transport layer and return a plain error (not *APIError)
strips the trailing slash before storing baseURL so URL construction does not produce double slashes

user_story.goal: have a single authoritative definition of every domain object type in the apikit package
user_story.benefit: server-side handlers and the SDK client share the same structs with no duplication or compile-time conflicts

acceptance_criteria.action: defines all domain object structs — User, APIKeyMeta, APIKeyFull, PAT, PATFull, Organization, OAuthProvider, AuthCallbackRequest, AuthCallbackResponse, CreateUserRequest, UpdateUserRequest, CreateTokenRequest, CreateOrgRequest, UpdateOrgRequest, HealthResponse, VersionResponse, RevokeKeyResponse, ListUsersOptions, ListOrgsOptions — as the single authoritative definitions in the apikit package; no parallel or internal-only equivalents are defined elsewhere in the package
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: each struct type appears exactly once in the apikit package

acceptance_criteria.action: uses snake_case json struct tags matching the OpenAPI schema field names on every struct field
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON marshaling produces field names matching the OpenAPI spec

acceptance_criteria.action: uses *time.Time for nullable timestamp fields (expires_at, revoked_at) so that null JSON values deserialize as nil
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: null JSON timestamps deserialize to nil *time.Time; non-null timestamps deserialize to valid *time.Time in RFC 3339

acceptance_criteria.action: defines FullName as a plain string (not *string) with json tag "full_name" and no omitempty, so every PATCH request body always includes the full_name field
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON body always contains "full_name" key regardless of value

acceptance_criteria.action: applies omitempty to optional request fields (AuthCallbackRequest.Expires *int, CreateTokenRequest.Expires *int, CreateOrgRequest.URL *string, UpdateOrgRequest.Name *string, UpdateOrgRequest.URL *string) so they are omitted from the JSON body when nil
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil optional fields are absent from serialized JSON

acceptance_criteria.action: returns []*User directly; OrgMember is not a distinct Go type in the apikit package
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*User slice; no OrgMember type defined

user_story.goal: have a single internal do method that handles all HTTP request construction, header setting, execution, and response dispatching
user_story.benefit: endpoint methods stay thin and all cross-cutting concerns (auth, content type, error handling) are applied consistently

acceptance_criteria.action: sets Accept: application/json on every outgoing request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request header Accept equals "application/json"

acceptance_criteria.action: sets Content-Type: application/json on the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request header Content-Type equals "application/json"
acceptance_criteria.state: the request has a non-nil body

acceptance_criteria.action: sets Authorization: Bearer <apiKey> on the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request header Authorization equals "Bearer <apiKey>"

acceptance_criteria.action: sets X-Request-ID: <requestID> on the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request header X-Request-ID equals the configured requestID

acceptance_criteria.action: does not set any Authorization header on the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request has no Authorization header

acceptance_criteria.action: does not set any X-Request-ID header on the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request has no X-Request-ID header

acceptance_criteria.action: passes the caller-supplied context.Context to the request via req.WithContext(ctx) before executing it
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request context matches caller-supplied ctx

acceptance_criteria.action: decodes the JSON response body into the typed response struct and returns it
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: typed struct populated from JSON; no error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server responds with HTTP 200 or 201

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a nil body with no error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil body; nil error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server responds with HTTP 204

acceptance_criteria.action: returns nil for the *Response[T] value and the sentinel error ErrNotModified; response headers from the 304 are not accessible
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil *Response[T]; ErrNotModified error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server responds with HTTP 304

acceptance_criteria.action: decodes the nested {"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}} envelope and returns an *APIError with Code and Message populated
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *APIError with Code set to the HTTP status code value from envelope and Message from envelope; err.Error() returns "API error {Code}: {Message}"
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server responds with a 4xx or 5xx status and a valid JSON error envelope

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an *APIError with Code set to the HTTP status code and Message set to the HTTP status text
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *APIError with Code from HTTP status and Message from HTTP status text
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server responds with a 4xx or 5xx status and a non-JSON or non-decodable body

acceptance_criteria.action: applies any per-request RequestOption functions (e.g., WithIfNoneMatch) to the *http.Request before execution
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request headers reflect applied RequestOption values

edge_cases.action: returns a plain error wrapping the JSON decode error; the original error is accessible via errors.Unwrap; does not return *APIError
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error; errors.Unwrap returns non-nil underlying JSON error; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false
edge_cases.error_condition: the server returns HTTP 200 or 201 with an empty, malformed, or non-JSON body (including plain text or HTML)

edge_cases.action: returns a plain error wrapping the JSON marshal error before any HTTP request is made; the original error is accessible via errors.Unwrap
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error; errors.Unwrap returns non-nil underlying marshal error
edge_cases.error_condition: the request body JSON encoding fails (marshal error)

edge_cases.action: returns a plain error wrapping the underlying network error; does not return *APIError; errors.Unwrap works on the returned error
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false; errors.Unwrap returns non-nil
edge_cases.error_condition: a network failure occurs during HTTP transport (connection refused, DNS resolution failure, etc.)

edge_cases.action: returns a plain error wrapping context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded; does not return *APIError; errors.Unwrap works on the returned error
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error wrapping context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false
edge_cases.error_condition: the caller-supplied context.Context is cancelled or its deadline is exceeded before or during the request

edge_cases.action: the HTTP transport layer fails and returns a plain error; the SDK does not panic; the error is not *APIError
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error from HTTP transport; no panic; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false
edge_cases.error_condition: Client was constructed with an empty baseURL and a request is issued

requirements.title: URL construction and mount point routing

user_story.goal: have all API requests routed to the correct URL paths relative to the server's root and configured mount point
user_story.benefit: I can deploy the server at any mount point and the SDK constructs correct URLs automatically

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs API endpoint URLs as baseURL + mountPoint + path (e.g., https://api.example.com/api/v1/user)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL equals baseURL + mountPoint + path

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs health probe endpoint URLs as baseURL + path without the mountPoint (e.g., https://api.example.com/healthz, https://api.example.com/readyz, https://api.example.com/version)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Healthz, Readyz, and Version request URLs equal baseURL + "/healthz", "/readyz", "/version" respectively

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs auth endpoint URLs as baseURL + mountPoint + path (e.g., baseURL + /api/v1/auth/providers)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: GetProviders and ExchangeOAuthCode request URLs include the mountPoint prefix

acceptance_criteria.action: uses the custom (normalized) mountPoint in all API and auth endpoint URL construction
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all non-health-probe request URLs use the custom mountPoint
acceptance_criteria.trigger: WithMountPoint is used to set a custom mount point

acceptance_criteria.action: interpolates path parameters (keyID, userID, tokenID, orgID, id) into URL paths as-is without calling url.PathEscape; callers are responsible for providing URL-safe values
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: path parameters appear verbatim in constructed URLs

edge_cases.action: the trailing slash stripping in NewClient ensures double slashes cannot appear in constructed URLs
edge_cases.return_contract: no double slashes in any constructed URL
edge_cases.error_condition: baseURL has a trailing slash that was not stripped (defensive check)

user_story.goal: perform conditional GET requests using ETags and detect 304 Not Modified responses
user_story.benefit: I can avoid redundant data transfer and processing when a resource has not changed since I last fetched it

acceptance_criteria.action: adds an If-None-Match header with the provided etag value to the request when applied as a RequestOption
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request header If-None-Match equals the provided etag string

acceptance_criteria.action: returns nil for the *Response[User] and the sentinel error ErrNotModified
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil *Response[User]; ErrNotModified; errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) returns true
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a WithIfNoneMatch RequestOption and the server returns HTTP 304

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a non-nil *Response[User] with Data populated, StatusCode set to 200, and Header containing the ETag header
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Response[User] non-nil; Response.ETag() returns the ETag header value; Response.StatusCode equals 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server returns HTTP 200 with an ETag response header

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the value of the ETag response header, or an empty string if the header is absent
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: string; empty string when ETag header is absent

acceptance_criteria.action: supports variadic ...RequestOption on GetUser, GetUserByID, GetToken, and GetOrg methods; all other methods do not accept RequestOption parameters
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: only the four listed methods accept ...RequestOption

acceptance_criteria.action: sets StatusCode to the actual HTTP status code returned by the server on any 2xx response (e.g., 201 for creation endpoints when wrapped in Response[T])
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response.StatusCode reflects the exact HTTP status code from the server

edge_cases.action: the *Response[T] is nil on 304; response headers from the 304 are not accessible via this API; callers must check errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) before dereferencing
edge_cases.error_condition: the server returns HTTP 304 and the caller attempts to access Response[T]

user_story.goal: distinguish server-side API errors from infrastructure failures and inspect error details programmatically
user_story.benefit: I can handle 404 Not Found differently from a network timeout in my application logic

acceptance_criteria.action: implements the error interface via an Error() method that returns the string "API error {Code}: {Message}" (e.g., "API error 404: User not found")
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Error() returns string matching format "API error %d: %s"

acceptance_criteria.action: contains Code int and Message string fields matching the server's error envelope fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: APIError.Code and APIError.Message are accessible to callers

acceptance_criteria.action: decodes the inner error object and returns an *APIError; callers retrieve it via errors.As(err, &apiErr)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *APIError with Code and Message from the inner envelope object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server returns a 4xx or 5xx response with a valid JSON error envelope {"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}}

acceptance_criteria.action: is a package-level sentinel error value; errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) returns true when the server returned HTTP 304
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: errors.Is(ErrNotModified, ErrNotModified) returns true

acceptance_criteria.action: wraps all plain errors (network failures, context errors, JSON decode/encode errors) so that errors.Unwrap returns the underlying cause
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: errors.Unwrap(err) returns non-nil for all plain error categories

edge_cases.action: returns an *APIError with Code set to the HTTP status code integer and Message set to the HTTP status text string; does not attempt to parse the HTML
edge_cases.return_contract: *APIError; Code from HTTP status; Message from HTTP status text (e.g., "Bad Gateway")
edge_cases.error_condition: the server returns a 4xx or 5xx status with an HTML body (e.g., a proxy error page)

edge_cases.action: returns an *APIError with Code set to the HTTP status code and Message set to the HTTP status text; the plain-text body is not used as the message
edge_cases.return_contract: *APIError; Code from HTTP status; Message from HTTP status text
edge_cases.error_condition: the server returns a 4xx or 5xx status with a plain-text body

edge_cases.action: errors.As returns false; the APIError is not assignable to unrelated error types
edge_cases.return_contract: errors.As returns false for incompatible target types
edge_cases.error_condition: caller uses errors.As with a non-*APIError target type

user_story.goal: call typed Go methods for all authenticated user endpoints without constructing raw HTTP requests
user_story.benefit: I get compile-time type safety and automatic error handling for all personal account operations

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/user" and returns *Response[User] with the decoded user and HTTP headers on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Response[User] non-nil with Data set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a PATCH request to mountPoint + "/user" with the JSON-encoded request body (always including full_name) and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and *UpdateUserRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/user/keys" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*APIKeyMeta
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*APIKeyMeta (empty slice not nil when server returns []); nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/user/keys/{keyID}/refresh" and returns *APIKeyFull on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *APIKeyFull non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and keyID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/user/keys/{keyID}" and returns *RevokeKeyResponse on success (HTTP 200)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *RevokeKeyResponse non-nil with KeyID and RevokedAt; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and keyID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/user/tokens" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*PAT
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*PAT (empty slice not nil when server returns []); nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/user/tokens" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *PATFull on success (HTTP 201)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *PATFull non-nil; nil error on 201
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and *CreateTokenRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/user/tokens/{tokenID}" and returns *Response[PAT] on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Response[PAT] non-nil with Data set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and tokenID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/user/tokens/{tokenID}" and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and tokenID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/user/orgs" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*Organization
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*Organization (empty slice not nil when server returns []); nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

edge_cases.error_condition: server returns an empty JSON array []

edge_cases.action: returns an *APIError or plain error per the do method's error classification rules
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error; nil for success only on 204
edge_cases.error_condition: server returns a non-204 error status

user_story.goal: call typed Go methods for all administrative user management endpoints
user_story.benefit: I can manage users, keys, and tokens on behalf of other users with full type safety

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/users" with query parameter include_blocked=true appended using url.Values
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL contains "include_blocked=true"; []*User returned on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a non-nil *ListUsersOptions where IncludeBlocked is true

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/users" without any include_blocked query parameter
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL contains no include_blocked parameter; []*User returned on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a nil *ListUsersOptions or with IncludeBlocked false

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}" and returns *Response[User] on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Response[User] non-nil with Data set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and user id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/users" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *User on success (HTTP 201)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 201
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and *CreateUserRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a PATCH request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, user id string, and *UpdateUserRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}/promote" with no body and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and user id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}/demote" with no body and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and user id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}/block" with no body and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and user id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/users/{id}/unblock" with no body and returns *User on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *User non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and user id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/users/{userID}/keys" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*APIKeyMeta
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*APIKeyMeta; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and userID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/users/{userID}/keys/{keyID}" and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, userID string, and keyID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/users/{userID}/tokens" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*PAT
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*PAT; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and userID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/users/{userID}/tokens/{tokenID}" and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, userID string, and tokenID string

edge_cases.error_condition: server returns an empty JSON array []

user_story.goal: call typed Go methods for all administrative organization management endpoints
user_story.benefit: I can create, manage, and delete organizations and their members with full type safety

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/orgs" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *Organization on success (HTTP 201)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Organization non-nil; nil error on 201
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and *CreateOrgRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/orgs" with query parameter include_blocked=true appended using url.Values
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL contains "include_blocked=true"; []*Organization returned on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a non-nil *ListOrgsOptions where IncludeBlocked is true

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/orgs" without any include_blocked query parameter
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL contains no include_blocked parameter; []*Organization returned on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with nil *ListOrgsOptions or IncludeBlocked false

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{id}" and returns *Response[Organization] on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Response[Organization] non-nil with Data set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and org id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a PATCH request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{id}" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *Organization on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Organization non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, org id string, and *UpdateOrgRequest

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{id}" with no body and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and org id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{id}/block" with no body and returns *Organization on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Organization non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and org id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{id}/unblock" with no body and returns *Organization on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *Organization non-nil; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and org id string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{orgID}/members" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*User
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*User; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and orgID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a PUT request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{orgID}/members/{userID}" with no request body and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, orgID string, and userID string

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a DELETE request to mountPoint + "/orgs/{orgID}/members/{userID}" with no body and returns nil error on success (HTTP 204)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: nil error on 204
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context, orgID string, and userID string

edge_cases.action: the SDK does not escape the value; the resulting malformed URL causes a transport-layer error; the caller is responsible for providing URL-safe IDs
edge_cases.return_contract: plain error from HTTP transport; no *APIError
edge_cases.error_condition: userID path parameter is not URL-safe

user_story.role: SDK consumer implementing an OAuth login flow
user_story.goal: call typed Go methods for OAuth provider discovery and code exchange
user_story.benefit: I can build the OAuth login flow in the CLI without constructing raw HTTP requests

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to mountPoint + "/auth/providers" and decodes the bare JSON array response into []*OAuthProvider
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: []*OAuthProvider; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a POST request to mountPoint + "/auth/callback" with the JSON-encoded request body and returns *AuthCallbackResponse on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *AuthCallbackResponse non-nil with User and APIKey populated; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context and *AuthCallbackRequest

edge_cases.action: returns an *APIError with Code and Message from the server error envelope
edge_cases.error_condition: server returns a 4xx error (e.g., invalid code or provider)

user_story.role: SDK consumer monitoring a deployed server
user_story.goal: call typed Go methods for health probe and version endpoints that bypass the mount point
user_story.benefit: I can check server liveness, readiness, and version information without knowing the API mount point

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to baseURL + "/healthz" (without mountPoint) and returns *HealthResponse on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *HealthResponse non-nil with Status set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to baseURL + "/readyz" (without mountPoint) and returns *HealthResponse on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *HealthResponse non-nil with Status set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a GET request to baseURL + "/version" (without mountPoint) and returns *VersionResponse on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: *VersionResponse non-nil with Version, BuildTime, Commit, and MountPoint set; nil error on 200
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid context

edge_cases.action: returns a plain error wrapping the network error; does not return *APIError
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil plain error; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false

user_story.role: SDK maintainer adding new filter fields in future iterations
user_story.goal: have list endpoint query parameters constructed via url.Values so new fields can be added without restructuring
user_story.benefit: ListUsersOptions and ListOrgsOptions can grow with pagination and search fields without breaking existing callers

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs query parameters using url.Values internally; when IncludeBlocked is true appends include_blocked=true; when false or options is nil appends nothing
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL has include_blocked=true only when IncludeBlocked is true

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs query parameters using url.Values internally; when IncludeBlocked is true appends include_blocked=true; when false or options is nil appends nothing
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request URL has include_blocked=true only when IncludeBlocked is true

acceptance_criteria.action: is defined as a struct with at minimum IncludeBlocked bool, designed to accommodate additional filter fields in future iterations without breaking changes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: struct compiles and is accepted by ListUsers as a pointer parameter

acceptance_criteria.action: is defined as a struct with at minimum IncludeBlocked bool, designed to accommodate additional filter fields in future iterations without breaking changes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: struct compiles and is accepted by ListOrgs as a pointer parameter

requirements.title: List endpoint bare JSON array decoding

user_story.goal: decode all list endpoint responses from bare JSON arrays rather than wrapped objects
user_story.benefit: the SDK correctly matches the OpenAPI spec which defines list responses as top-level arrays

acceptance_criteria.action: decodes list endpoint responses directly into []T without an intermediate wrapper struct, matching the bare JSON array format defined in the OpenAPI spec
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON array [{...},{...}] decoded into Go slice with all elements populated

acceptance_criteria.action: decodes the response into an empty (non-nil) Go slice
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil empty slice; nil error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the server returns an empty JSON array [] for a list endpoint

edge_cases.action: the JSON decode fails and returns a plain error wrapping the decode error; the SDK does not attempt to unwrap nested arrays
edge_cases.error_condition: the server returns a wrapped JSON object (e.g., {"keys": [...]}) instead of a bare array for a list endpoint

user_story.goal: have the SDK follow HTTP redirects automatically using Go's default policy
user_story.benefit: requests succeed without extra client-side handling in the common case; callers needing custom redirect logic can inject their own *http.Client

acceptance_criteria.action: uses Go's default HTTP redirect policy, following up to 10 redirects automatically via the configured httpClient
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: requests are redirected up to 10 times before a redirect error is returned

acceptance_criteria.action: supports callers injecting a configured *http.Client with a custom CheckRedirect policy via WithHTTPClient
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: custom redirect policy honored; SDK does not override it

user_story.goal: import the SDK from the root apikit package without any sub-package import path
user_story.benefit: I get both the server framework and the client library from a single import

acceptance_criteria.action: places all SDK source files (sdk.go, sdk_types.go, sdk_user.go, sdk_admin.go, sdk_auth.go, sdk_health.go, sdk_errors.go, sdk_test.go, sdk_integration_test.go) in the root apikit package (package apikit); no SDK code is placed under internal/
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all SDK types and functions are importable by external consumers as package apikit

acceptance_criteria.action: requires Go 1.18 or later as the minimum version (floor) due to generics support required by Response[T]; the go.mod go directive is set to the latest stable release at implementation time
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: package compiles with Go 1.18+

correctness_properties.title: Client immutability guarantees concurrent safety
correctness_properties.invariant: no field on c is modified after NewClient returns; all concurrent calls to any method on c observe the same field values set during construction

correctness_properties.title: APIError classification is exhaustive for non-2xx non-304 responses
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP response with status code S where S >= 400
correctness_properties.invariant: do always returns a non-nil *APIError (never a plain error) for any response with status code >= 400; Code is always set to S

correctness_properties.title: Plain errors always wrap their underlying cause
correctness_properties.for_any: plain error returned by do (network, context, JSON decode, JSON encode)
correctness_properties.invariant: errors.Unwrap(err) is non-nil for every plain error returned by do; the original cause is always preserved

correctness_properties.title: Mount point normalization is idempotent
correctness_properties.for_any: any path string p passed to WithMountPoint
correctness_properties.invariant: the stored mountPoint always starts with exactly one "/" and has no trailing "/"; applying WithMountPoint twice with the same value produces the same result

correctness_properties.title: Health probes always bypass mount point
correctness_properties.for_any: any Client c regardless of its mountPoint configuration
correctness_properties.invariant: Healthz, Readyz, and Version always construct URLs as c.baseURL + "/healthz", "/readyz", "/version" respectively, never prefixing c.mountPoint

correctness_properties.title: List endpoints return non-nil empty slice for empty server response
correctness_properties.for_any: any list endpoint method when the server returns []
correctness_properties.invariant: the returned Go slice is non-nil and has length 0; it is never nil

correctness_properties.title: Authorization header is sent iff apiKey is non-empty
correctness_properties.for_any: any request made by any Client method
correctness_properties.invariant: the Authorization header is present with value "Bearer <apiKey>" if and only if Client.apiKey is non-empty; when apiKey is empty, no Authorization header is set

correctness_properties.title: ErrNotModified is returned iff the server responds with 304
correctness_properties.for_any: any conditional GET method (GetUser, GetUserByID, GetToken, GetOrg)
correctness_properties.invariant: ErrNotModified is returned if and only if the server response status code is 304; on 304 the accompanying *Response[T] is always nil

correctness_properties.title: UpdateUserRequest always includes full_name in JSON body
correctness_properties.for_any: any call to UpdateUser or UpdateUserByID with any UpdateUserRequest value
correctness_properties.invariant: the JSON-encoded request body always contains the "full_name" key, even when FullName is an empty string, because the field has no omitempty tag

correctness_properties.title: Canonical types are defined exactly once in the apikit package
correctness_properties.for_any: any domain object type name listed in sdk_types.go (User, Organization, APIKeyMeta, etc.)
correctness_properties.invariant: each type name appears in exactly one Go file in the apikit package; no duplicate or shadow definitions exist

execution_paths.title: Successful authenticated GET with ETag capture and conditional re-fetch

steps.action: calls apikit.NewClient(baseURL, WithAPIKey(key)) to obtain a *Client
strips trailing slash from baseURL, sets default mountPoint /api/v1, stores apiKey, returns *Client
calls client.GetUser(ctx) to fetch the authenticated user
constructs URL as baseURL + /api/v1 + /user, sets Accept: application/json and Authorization: Bearer <key> headers, executes GET via httpClient
returns HTTP 200 with JSON User body and ETag response header
decodes JSON body into User struct, populates *Response[User] with Data, StatusCode=200, and Header containing ETag, returns to caller
reads etag := resp.ETag() from the response; later calls client.GetUser(ctx, WithIfNoneMatch(etag))
constructs the same URL, applies WithIfNoneMatch RequestOption setting If-None-Match header, executes GET
returns HTTP 304 Not Modified
detects 304, returns nil *Response[User] and ErrNotModified to caller
checks errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) == true and uses cached User data
calls apikit.NewClient(baseURL, WithAPIKey(key)) to obtain a *Client
calls client.GetUserByID(ctx, "nonexistent-id") to fetch a user that does not exist
constructs URL as baseURL + /api/v1 + /users/nonexistent-id, sets headers, executes GET
returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error": {"code": 404, "message": "User not found"}}
reads 404 status, decodes the nested error envelope, constructs *APIError{Code: 404, Message: "User not found"}, returns it as error
calls errors.As(err, &apiErr) which returns true; reads apiErr.Code == 404 and apiErr.Message == "User not found"; reads err.Error() == "API error 404: User not found"

execution_paths.title: List organizations with include_blocked filter and empty result

steps.action: calls apikit.NewClient(baseURL, WithAPIKey(adminKey)) to obtain an admin *Client
calls client.ListOrgs(ctx, &ListOrgsOptions{IncludeBlocked: true})
constructs URL as baseURL + /api/v1 + /orgs, appends include_blocked=true query parameter via url.Values, executes GET
returns HTTP 200 with bare JSON array [] (no organizations match)
decodes bare JSON array [] into an empty (non-nil) []*Organization slice, returns to caller
receives empty []*Organization with nil error; len(orgs) == 0

execution_paths.title: Organization member management via admin methods

steps.action: calls apikit.NewClient(baseURL, WithAPIKey(adminKey)) to obtain an admin *Client
calls client.CreateOrg(ctx, &CreateOrgRequest{Name: "Acme", Slug: "acme"}) to create an organization
sends POST to baseURL + /api/v1/orgs with JSON body {"name":"Acme","slug":"acme"}
returns HTTP 201 with JSON Organization body
decodes JSON body into *Organization, returns to caller
calls client.AddOrgMember(ctx, org.ID, userID) to add a user to the organization
sends PUT to baseURL + /api/v1/orgs/{org.ID}/members/{userID} with no request body
returns HTTP 204 No Content
detects 204, returns nil error to caller
calls client.ListOrgMembers(ctx, org.ID) to verify the member was added
sends GET to baseURL + /api/v1/orgs/{org.ID}/members, decodes bare JSON array into []*User
returns HTTP 200 with bare JSON array containing the added user
receives []*User with the added member; confirms membership
calls apikit.NewClient("https://api.example.com", WithAPIKey(key), WithMountPoint("api/v2")) to obtain a *Client with custom mount point
normalizes "api/v2" by prepending "/" to produce "/api/v2" and stores it as mountPoint
calls client.GetUser(ctx)
constructs URL as https://api.example.com + /api/v2 + /user = "https://api.example.com/api/v2/user", executes GET
returns HTTP 200 with User JSON at the /api/v2 mount point
decodes response and returns *Response[User] to caller
calls client.Healthz(ctx) — health probe should bypass mount point
constructs URL as https://api.example.com + /healthz (no mount point), executes GET
returns HTTP 200 with HealthResponse JSON
receives *HealthResponse confirming health probe bypasses custom mount point

execution_paths.title: OAuth code exchange flow (CLI building block)

steps.action: calls apikit.NewClient(baseURL) (no API key yet) to create an unauthenticated client
calls client.GetProviders(ctx) to discover available OAuth providers
sends GET to baseURL + /api/v1/auth/providers, decodes bare JSON array into []*OAuthProvider
returns HTTP 200 with []*OAuthProvider JSON array
displays provider list to user; user selects a provider; CLI opens browser to provider.AuthorizeURL and receives OAuth code via redirect
calls client.ExchangeOAuthCode(ctx, &AuthCallbackRequest{Provider: "github", Code: code, RedirectURI: redirectURI})
sends POST to baseURL + /api/v1/auth/callback with JSON body, decodes *AuthCallbackResponse
returns HTTP 200 with AuthCallbackResponse containing User and APIKeyFull
receives *AuthCallbackResponse; stores resp.APIKey.Key as the API key for future requests; creates authenticated client with WithAPIKey(resp.APIKey.Key)

error_handling.condition: Server returns 4xx or 5xx with a valid JSON error envelope {"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}}
error_handling.behavior: do decodes the inner error object and returns *APIError with Code and Message populated; APIError.Error() returns "API error {Code}: {Message}"; callers use errors.As(err, &apiErr) to inspect

error_handling.condition: Server returns 4xx or 5xx with a non-JSON body (HTML proxy error, plain text, binary)
error_handling.behavior: do returns *APIError with Code set to HTTP status code integer and Message set to HTTP status text string; the body content is not used as the message

error_handling.condition: Server returns HTTP 304 on a conditional GET
error_handling.behavior: do returns nil *Response[T] and the sentinel ErrNotModified; response headers from the 304 are not accessible; caller checks errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified)

error_handling.condition: Network failure during HTTP transport (connection refused, DNS failure, TLS error)
error_handling.behavior: do returns a plain error wrapping the underlying network error; errors.Unwrap returns the underlying cause; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false

error_handling.condition: Caller-supplied context.Context is cancelled or deadline exceeded before or during request
error_handling.behavior: do returns a plain error wrapping context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded; errors.Unwrap returns the cause; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false

error_handling.condition: Server returns 2xx with an empty, malformed, or non-JSON response body
error_handling.behavior: do returns a plain error wrapping the JSON decode error; errors.Unwrap returns non-nil; does not return *APIError

error_handling.condition: Request body JSON encoding fails (marshal error) before any HTTP request is made
error_handling.behavior: do returns a plain error wrapping the JSON marshal error; errors.Unwrap returns non-nil; no HTTP request is sent

error_handling.condition: Client was constructed with an empty baseURL and a request is issued
error_handling.behavior: the HTTP transport layer fails and returns a plain error; the SDK does not panic; errors.As(err, &apiErr) returns false

symbols.name: DefaultClient
symbols.notes: Used as the default httpClient when no WithHTTPClient option is provided

symbols.name: NewRequest
symbols.signature: func NewRequest(method, url string, body io.Reader) (*Request, error)
symbols.notes: Used internally by do to construct requests; prefer NewRequestWithContext for context propagation

symbols.name: NewRequestWithContext
symbols.signature: func NewRequestWithContext(ctx context.Context, method, url string, body io.Reader) (*Request, error)
symbols.notes: Preferred form for context-aware request construction; passes ctx directly

symbols.name: Client.Do
symbols.notes: Executes the HTTP request; used by the SDK's do method via the configured httpClient

symbols.name: Response
symbols.signature: type Response struct { Status string; StatusCode int; Header Header; Body io.ReadCloser; ... }
symbols.notes: HTTP response; Body must be closed after reading

symbols.name: Header
symbols.notes: Used in Response[T].Header to expose response headers including ETag

symbols.name: NewServer
symbols.notes: Used in integration tests to create a mock HTTP server

symbols.name: Server.URL
symbols.notes: The base URL of the test server, passed as baseURL to NewClient in integration tests

symbols.name: Server.Close
symbols.notes: Shuts down the test server; called via defer in integration tests

symbols.name: Values
symbols.notes: Used for query parameter construction in ListUsers and ListOrgs

symbols.name: Values.Set
symbols.notes: Sets a query parameter key-value pair

symbols.name: Values.Encode
symbols.notes: Encodes the values into URL query string format (key=value&...)

symbols.name: Marshal
symbols.notes: Used to encode request bodies

symbols.name: NewDecoder
symbols.notes: Used to stream-decode response bodies

symbols.name: Decoder.Decode
symbols.notes: Decodes a JSON value from the reader into v

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Used to define the ErrNotModified sentinel

symbols.name: As
symbols.signature: func As(err error, target any) bool
symbols.notes: Used by callers to extract *APIError from returned errors

symbols.name: Is
symbols.notes: Used by callers to check for ErrNotModified

symbols.name: Unwrap
symbols.notes: Used to verify that plain errors wrap their underlying cause

symbols.name: Sprintf
symbols.notes: Used in APIError.Error() to format the error string

symbols.name: Errorf
symbols.notes: Used with %w verb to wrap underlying errors in plain error returns
